zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry le Moyns II於1376年出生在英國素瑞時，他的父親Henry Moyne I25歲，母親Mrs. Henry Moyne I23歲。他於1400年與Ann Scudamore在英國英格蘭素瑞結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1402年在他的家鄉享年26歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry le Moyns II于1376年出生在英国素瑞时，他的父亲Henry Moyne I25岁，母亲Mrs. Henry Moyne I23岁。他于1400年与Ann Scudamore在英国英格兰素瑞结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1402年在他的家乡享年26岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Moyns
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Fife) and English: from the Middle English personal name Moise, Mose, Mosse, a vernacular form (from Old French Moïse) of Moses .

variants.name: Moses
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, African American, and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Biblical personal name borne by the Israelite leader who led the Israelites out of Egypt, as related in the Book of Exodus. The Hebrew form of the name is Moshe . It is probably of Egyptian origin, from a short form of an ancient Egyptian personal name such as Rameses or Tutmosis, meaning ‘conceived (by a god)’. However, very early in its history the name acquired a folk etymology, being taken as a derivative of the Hebrew root verb mshh ‘extract or draw (something), e.g. from water’ and was associated with a story of the infant Moses being discovered among the bullrushes by Pharaoh's daughter (Exodus 2: 1-10). As a Welsh surname, it was adopted among Dissenter families in the 18th and 19th centuries. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Italian Moise , Hungarian Mózes (see Mozes ), Assyrian/Chaldean Moshe , Arabic Musa .
English: variant of Moss , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
English: variant of Moyses, a Cornish personal name derived from Middle English Moises, a vernacular form of Moses (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry le Moyns II was born in 1376, in Surrey, England, his father, Henry Moyne I, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. Henry Moyne I, was 23. He married Ann Scudamore in 1400, in Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1402, in his hometown, at the age of 26.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry le Moyns II est né en 1376, à Surrey, Angleterre, son père, Henry Moyne I, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Henry Moyne I, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Ann Scudamore en 1400, à Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1402, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 26 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry le Moyns II 1376 in Surrey, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Moyne I 25 und seine Mutter Mrs. Henry Moyne I 23. Er hat 1400 in Surrey, England, United Kingdom Ann Scudamore geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1402 im Alter von 26 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry le Moyns II è nato il nel 1376 nel Surrey, England, il suo padre, Henry Moyne I, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Henry Moyne I, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Ann Scudamore nel 1400, nel Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1402, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 26 anni.

ja.Betchworth, Surrey, England: イギリススリーベトチウオルス
ja.lifeSummary: Henry le Moyns IIが1376年にイギリススリーで生まれた時，父Henry Moyne Iは25歳，母Mrs. Henry Moyne Iは23歳であった。1400年にイギリスイギリススリーでAnn Scudamoreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1402年に故郷で26歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry le Moyns II(이)가 1376년 영국 수리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Moyne I(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Henry Moyne I(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1400년 영국 잉글랜드 수리에서 Ann Scudamore(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1402년 그의 고향에서 26세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry le Moyns II nasceu em 1376, em Surrey, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Moyne I, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Henry Moyne I, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Ann Scudamore em 1400, em Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1402, em sua cidade natal, com 26 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry le Moyns II родился в 1376 г. в местности сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Moyne I было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Henry Moyne I, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Scudamore в 1400 г. в местности сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1402 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 26 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry le Moyns II nació en 1376, en Surrey, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Moyne I, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. Henry Moyne I, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Ann Scudamore en 1400, en Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1402, en su pueblo, a la edad de 26 años.
